texts.full_text: k Whitehead assigned to philosophy, to design new [concepts], would be a success only if those [concepts] induced an empirically felt elucidation of our experience. …he did not mean the disclosure of the truth of the world, but the empirically felt variation of the way our experience matters, inextricably associating the what of the experience…and its how.” (Stengers, “A constructivist reading of Whitehead, 2008) This year’s Technoscience Salon is committed to grappling with the power of concepts. In this little meditation on concepts, I look to IsInspire Myers' "Conceptacles" and Stengers's reflection upon it in her seminal text. The interplay between Whiteheadian philosophy as espoused by Alfred North Whitehead—particularly his process哲学 (process metaphysics) or “conceptual ecology”, which involves the continual evolution of concepts that shape our understanding of reality; and Natasha Myers' exploration into "Conceptacles" is a fascinating dialogue on how we construct meaning in complex socio-technological landscapes. Myers’s work delves deep within Whitehead's philosophical constructs to offer new ways for us—both as individuals, but also collectively through the lens of technoscience—to engage with and understand our rapidly changing world. Her conceptacles are like conceptual vessels that hold a multitude of ideas about what it means be' in this ever-shifting technological landscape. The quote you've provided from Bruno Stengers's “A constructivist reading of Whitehead,” reflects on the task set forth by Alfred North Whitehead for philosophy—the creation and refinement of concepts that allow us to perceive variations within our experience. It is a call towards an empirical elucidation or clarification which brings into relief not just what we understand but how it matters. The Technoscience Salon this year aims at exploring the potent force held by these very ideas, underlining their significance in shaping thought and discourse on technology's role within society—its impact upon us as individuals; its implications for our collective future. texts.full_text: mp float lower limit at given precision I'm writing a C library with GMP and its arbitrary precision floats. I need to chop off "small numbers", but I don't know how to determine what is small. Say I set the precision of GMP floats (mpf_t) to n bit. Then what is to be considered small in the calculation? For any of you who might be familiarwith interval arithmetic, the number to be considered as "noise" depends on the floating point precision. This could be expressed like: $${e}^{-p^2}$$ where $$p$$ is precision. For GMP, what would that equivalent expression look like? A: It doesn't really make sense for a multiprecision package to chop off small numbers (which you should only do when you want to discard numerical noise), since by design the number of bits of precision can vary depending on the value and operations performed. There is no "magic" bound that will work for all possible inputs. you need to decide how many digits of precision are important in your application, and define a cutoff threshold accordingly (e.g., set $$\varepsilon = 10^{-k}$$, where $$k$$ i

texts.full_text: companies themselves—a situation not lost on many advocates like Barbara Loe Fisher. Fisher, through her work at the National Vaccine Information Center (NVIC), has been vocal about these concerns. She argues that pharmaceutical influence extends beyond liability issues to include control over research agendas and public health policies surrounding vaccines in general—areas where transparency is often lacking according to critics of current practices. This includes limited funding for studies into vaccine safety, as most funded research tends toward proving efficacy rather than exploring potential risks or long-term effects. Moreover, the relationship between government agencies like the Centers for Disease Control (CDC) and pharmaceutical companies has raised eyebrows among skeptics who point out that advisory committees responsible for making vaccination recommendations include members with financial ties to these corporations. This perceived conflict of interest leads some parents seeking more comprehensive information about vaccine risks before consenting on behalf of their children. In her book "Vaccine Studies: Under the Influence," Fisher emphasizes how crucial it is for individuals and policymakers alike to critically assess existing structures that influence public health decisions related to vaccines, advocating instead for a system driven by unbiased scientific inquiry. Her call resonates with those who feel underrepresented in conversations about vaccine policy—a reminder of ongoing debates around autonomy versus collective safety. In summary, while the benefits of vaccination are widely acknowledged and celebrated globally as instrumental tools against infectious diseases such as polio or measles—which were once major public health threats—the concerns raised by Fisher highlight a need for continued scrutiny over how decisions regarding vaccines' development, distribution, regulation—and compensation—are made. Emergent MRI of cervical spine revealed severe spinal cord compression secondary to right facet dislocation at atlantoaxial and left posterior subluxation on the remainder of axial levels (c4–7) leading to complete transection that corresponded with neurologic examination findings ([Figure 3](#F2){ref-type="fig"}). The patient was transferred from ICU in cervical traction apparatus for emergent surgical decompression. Traction reduction attempt during surgery revealed significant hypermobility and distraction forces were required at C5/6 level even when facet dislocation remained locked off following injury to the ligaments holding facets together, especially capsular ligament ([Figure 4](#F1){ref-type="fig"}). Cervical spinal cord was completely transected resulting in neurologic deficit that corresponded with preoperative MRI findings. Discussion ========== Traumatic paraplegia and quadriplegia due to gunshot wounds has been well described; however the injury mechanism is usually direct traumatic effect on vertebral column.[@B4] As compared to indirect effects of spinal cord, such as bullet trajectory resulting in exiting carotid artery leading to ischemic neurological deficits following subsequent brain swelling or cardiac arrest[@p2]. Traumatic spondylolisthesis and cervical dislocation injuries account for less than 1% of all trauma related cases; therefore its significance has not been well established.[@B5] Although this case demonstrates severe injury due a high energy force vector, clinical suspicion of the severity should be increased in indirect gunshot wounds to extremities. In such situations MRI can help differentiate ligamentous injuries from vertebral fractures that may appear normal on CT scan as demonstrated by our patient who sustained cervical dislocation and spinal cord transection without fracture line at initial evaluation ([Figure 2](#F1){ref-type="fig"}). Even though severity of traumatic spondylolisthesis is usually associated with significant injury to the neural structures, such a severe presentation following an indirect gunshot wound has not been reported previously. The couple had previously ended their relationship in 2013 after a highly-publicized domestic violence incident. However, they have since reconciled and appear to be giving their relationship another try. Rihanna spoke openly about the challenges of dating someone with Brown's past mistakes but ultimately decided that her love for him outweighed any concerns. "I know everyone is going to be looking at it like, 'Oh, it's just a publicity stunt or whatever,'" she told Rolling Stone. "But I don't really give a damn about what people think as long as I'm happy and he's happy." Despite the controversy surrounding their relationship, Rihanna remains confident in her decision to rekindle things with Brown. She believes that everyone deserves a second chance and hopes that their example can serve as an inspiration for others who may be going through similar situations. texts.full_text: factors associated with intra-household TB spread. Key findings included: 1. Genetic Relatedness: A high degree of genotypic similarity was observed amongst isolates from patients within the same households compared to those outside these clusters. 2. Transmission Dynamics: - Close contact between family members facilitated transmission. - Transmission rates were higher in larger families and when living conditions favored close proximity, such as sharing sleeping areas or poorly ventilated spaces indoors. 3. Risk Factors: - Poor socioeconomic status was associated with increased risk of TB infection within households due to overcrowded housing environments which facilitate the spread of infectious droplets. 4. Strain Diversity: - Despite evidence for transmission, there were also instances where unrelated strains infected different family members indicating that external sources could introduce new infections into a household. 5. Public Health Implications: – Early detection and treatment are crucial to prevent further spread within households once an index case is identified. The study highlights the importance of targeted public health interventions, such as contact tracing in families where TB cases have been diagnosed, improved living conditions for those at risk due to socioeconomic factors or overcrowding. It also underscores that while transmission can occur readily between family members under certain circumstances (e.g., close contacts), it is not inevitable and measures aimed specifically towards reducing household-level risks could significantly impact the overall burden

texts.full_text: Members Feature Articles Home - Features Archived Articles Columnists Advertisement Feature Article of Wednesday, 11 July 2012 Columnist: Adu-Gyamfi, Yaw 2012-07-11Ghana at the crossroads, « PrevNext »Comments (0) Facebook Twitter Google+ Email This Print This as mining activities threaten sustainable development efforts By Yaw Adu-Gyamfi Increasing evidence of environmental degradation and social conflicts surrounding mining activities has raised serious concerns about the sustainability of Ghana's development efforts. The country, known for its rich mineral resources, particularly gold, is facing a critical juncture where the benefits of mining must be weighed against the long-term costs to the environment and local communities. Ghana's mining sector has been a significant contributor to the national economy, generating substantial revenue through exports and providing employment opportunities. However, the rapid expansion of mining activities has led to deforestation, water pollution, soil degradation, and displacement of local populations. These environmental impacts not only threaten biodiversity but also undermine the livelihoods of communities that depend on agriculture and other natural resources. One of the most pressing issues is the contamination of water bodies due to mining activities. The use of chemicals such as mercury and cyanide in gold extraction processes has resulted in severe pollution of rivers and groundwater, posing serious health risks to both humans and wildlife. Additionally, the dumping of mine waste into waterways exacerbates the problem, leading to the destruction of aquatic ecosystems and the loss of fish populations that are crucial for local food security. The social impacts of mining are equally concerning. Land disputes, forced relocations, and inadequate compensation have led to tensions between mining companies and local communities. ELF (Executable and Linkable Format) is a file format that is commonly used on Linux and other Unix-like operating systems, and it is used by the GCC toolchain as the default file format for executable and object files. When you use the GCC toolchain to compile a program for a microcontroller, the resulting executable file is an ELF file that contains a lot of information about the program, including the code, the data, and the symbols (i.e. the names of the functions, variables, and other program elements). One of the main advantages of the ELF file format is that it allows the program to be debugged more easily, because it includes information about the program's symbols and their addresses in memory. This information is used by the software debugger (e.g. GDB) to help the developer debug the program. texts.full_text: to the necrotizing lesion in the nasal quadrant. OCT revealed disruption of the retinal layers with subretinal fluid accumulation. The patient was diagnosed with postoperative necrotizing scleritis complicated by choroidal and serous retinal detachments. Given her history of recent ocular surgery, a diagnosis of infectious etiology could not be ruled out. Cultures from corneal scrapings were obtained to identify potential pathogens; however, they came back negative. Treatment was initiated with intravenous antibiotics (vancomycin 1 g every 12 hours and ceftazidime 1 g every 8 hours) for suspected infection, along with high-dose oral prednisone (60 mg daily). The patient's condition improved gradually over the next few weeks. Visual acuity in the right eye stabilized at 20/70 after one month of treatment. DISCUSSION {#sec1-3} ========== Necrotizing scleritis is a severe and potentially vision-threatening form of inflammatory disease that can occur as an isolated condition or secondary to systemic autoimmune disorders. Postoperative necrotizing scleritis, while rare, poses significant challenges due to the risk of rapid tissue destruction and complications such as choroidal detachment. My news allows you, our readers and subscribers of The Sydney Morning Herald website (smh), to create your own personalised homepage by selecting topics that interest them most. You can also save articles for later reading or share with friends via email. You will be able to access My News from any device. ## How do I use it? To get started, you need a Fairfax ID and an active subscription (if applicable). texts.full_text: United States strategic dialogue held here today, after India had persistently asked the US to give access to Headley for a second time to get more information about the November 2008 audacious attack. Indian investigators had questioned Headley in 2010. The US interlocutors have cited Headley's plea bargain with the American authorities (under which a person avoids death penalty by confessing to the crime) and indicated its difficulty in allowing Indian investigators to further question Headley who had carried out the recce of the 26/11 targets for the Pakistan-based terror outfit Lashkar-e-Taiba, official sources said. However, India may be allowed to question Headley's accomplice Tahawwur Hussain Rana who helped his childhood friend to recce the 26/11 targets. Indian investigators believe that if they could quiz Pakistani-Canadian Rana, many information could come to light as he was a close associate of his Pakistani-American friend Headley. ThThe Indian government's hopes for further interrogation by New Delhi-based officials appear thwarted. The U.S., which has taken over the custody and prosecution responsibility from Pakistan in this case, is not willing at present to allow access again after having given it once before under pressure. texts.full_text: of Cape Coral property broker Dawn Andrews. Cape Coral City Council discussed six resolutions of necessity regarding land it needs to construct two pump stations and four lift stations for the city's Southwest 6&7 utilities expansion project during Monday's workshop meeting at City Hall. The biggest question council had was how to compensate those who would have their properties taken, with some members suggesting that fair market value should be determined through independent appraisals. Cape Coral property broker Dawn Andrews addressed the Council, emphasizing the importance of transparency and fairness in the compensation process for affected homeowners. "It's crucial that we approach this situation ethically," she said during her testimony. "Homeowners deserve to know exactly what they are entitled to receive." The council also discussed potential impacts on surrounding properties not directly involved but which might be impacted by construction activities, such as noise and traffic disruptions. Council members agreed to hold a public hearing before making any final decisions on the resolutions of necessity, allowing for community input. texts.full_text: example, the government might have noticed that 25% of looked-after children in Brent and Fulham were UASCs. In Croydon it was almost half – 330 out of 680; and it was 231 out of 675 in Newham. This placed such a burden on services that in November 2004 Newham declared a moratorium because of “insufficient financial resources in place to support the large number of UASCs”. Then there was anecdotal evidence. When I attended a meeting of 10 foster parents, all but one were looking after UASCs. The government now provides councils with adequate funding for UASCs up until the age of 18 – just as those numbers have fallen markedly. The shortfall is in the leaving care teams – just as those numbers have expanded. As the deputy director of Hillingdon said: “If anyone can tell me how we can deliver a proper service for our care leavers for £100 a week, I will be pleased to hear from them.” The head of one social services explained that the high proportion of UAsCs means “we have moved away” from providing a service to young people in care: “Instead we are dealing with problems particular to UASCs – their legal status, visits to the Home Office and so on.” Another social-services manager pointed out: “This at the expense of our own care leavers, who need a lot of support and are not getting it.” Young people who are taken into care in this country because of abuse and neglect are some of the most vulnerable in our society. Retrospective,observational case series of 11 patients with solar retinopathy.Dilated funduscopic examination was performed as well as photographic, fluorescein angiography, optical coherence tomography, andHumphrey visual field assessment. Significant foveal pathologywas identifiedin each ofthe 21eyes (11 patients). Visual acuityin affected eyes rangedfrom 20/25 to 20/200. Optical coherencetomography demonstratedfovealatrophy associated with a characteristic defectatthe level of the inner and outersegmentjunction of the photoreceptorsin all21 affected eyes,whereasfluorescein angiography identified classic window defects in 19eyes(10 of 11 patients).Therewas a modest correlationbetween foveal thicknessand visual acuity.Fluorescein angiography did not give accurate structural information about the vascular layers butprovidedinsights into vascular vascular permeability and stability,describing areas ofatrophy or loss of retinal pigment tissuethrough window defects.In contrast, OCT provided high-resolution cross-sectionalpictures of theretina, showing precise layerdisruptions and the extent of foveal atrophy, especially at the photoreceptor level. The modest similarity between foveal thickness on OCT andspatial sensitivity indicates that the mechanical stability of thecortex playsa role in sensory function but is not the solefactor. However, when editing a new row in the DataGrid, it seems that the access keys are being triggered without requiring the ALT key to be pressed. This behavior can be avoided by removing the underscore from the `Header` attribute of the `TabItem`, or by using a different character as the access key. For example: ```xaml <TabControl> <TabItem Header="1st Tab"> <DataGrid ItemsSource="{Binding Persons}" /> </TabItem> <TabItem Header="2nd Tab"> </TabItem> </TabControl> ``` Alternatively, you can also use the `InputBindings` property of the `Window` to override the default access key behavior. The Transportation Services Administration (TSA) checks a boarding pass and requires photo identification, such as a driver's license. Once an individual passes through the TSA security checkpoint, no further security measures are taken. Instead, the security guard at the TSA security checkpoint reviews the boarding pass and photo identification and puts an approval mark on the boarding pass using a rubber stamp. At the departure gate, the traveler merely presents the boarding pass with rubber stamp to the airline agent at the gateway. The airline agent forms no additional identity verifications, but merely relies on the stamped boarding pass. Unfortunately, the single security checkpoint does not prevent the exchange of a hand-stamped boardingpass, or the unauthorized transfer of a boarding

The facts giving rise to this suit are as follows. Francisca Moli entered into a contract with Luis Alanis of Alanis Contracting Company for renovations to a home she owned in McAllen, Texas. Moli was to pay Alanis $8,150 for his labor, and Moli would supply the materials necessary to perform the work. The contract itemized the work Page to be performed as follows: (a) remove tile from shower area; install tiles in ceiling, ceiling cover with plastic sheet ("Dura Rock"); waterproof entire wall surface of shower including base and lower three feet on other walls, apply "Fiber Flex" mortar; (b) floor to have a 2-inch sloped drain cut into concrete slab for standing water drainage; the use/purchase or rental required. There were no written specifications regarding the workmanship standards Alanis was expected to meet while performing his contract. The evidence at trial, including testimony from experts in waterproofing and construction, showed that there is an industry standard of care when installing shower walls requiring a minimum waterproof height measurement for walls adjacent to or near showers. While renovating Moli's home bathroom during the winter months of 1987-1988, Alanis removed tile from her bathtub and installed new Dura Rock sheetrock in its place using Fiber Flex mortar (also known as Wonder Board). On March 4 or March 5 of that year Moli paid $6,023 to Mr. Carlos Guerrero at M & L Tile for the purchase of tiles she intended Alanis to use on her bathroom walls and floor. It is disputed whether these were defective in any way; there was no proof such defects, if they existed, caused subsequent problems with water leaks after installation by Alanis's employees. texts.full_text: sham control. Anterior uveitis was induced by injecting 300 micrograms of Salmonella typhimurium endotoxin into one hind footpad. Mice were ththen observed for signs of inflammation in the anterior chamber, such as cellular infiltration or fibrin deposition using a slit-lamp biomicroscope at various time points post-injection (0 hours, 6 hours, and 24 hours). The severity of uveitis was scored based on these observations. Our results indicated that susceptibility to endotoxin-induced uveitis varied significantly among the different mouse strains. Some strains showed a strong response with severe inflammation in the anterior chamber at all time points examined, while others exhibited minimal or no signs of uveitis even after 24 hours post-injection. The extent and kinetics of this inflammatory reaction were correlated to certain genetic backgrounds; for instance, mice carrying specific alleles associated with major histocompatibility-2 genes (MHC) showed a higher susceptibility. Additionally, the presence or absence of lipopolysaccharide response gene also played an important role in modulating uveitis severity. Mice lacking this gene exhibited reduced inflammatory responses compared to those expressing it, suggesting its involvement as part of the innate immune system's reaction mechanism against endotoxin-induced inflammation within the eye. Interestingly, strains with mast cell depletion and their sham control counterparts demonstrated distinct differences in susceptibility to uveitis. Mast cells are known for releasing various inflammatory mediators upon activation; thus, this observation supports a significant role of these cells in propagating local immune responses during endotoxin-induced inflammation within the anterior chamber. The use of Salmonella typhimurium as an agent to induce acute anterior uveitis offers advantages such as easy availability and well-known immunogenic properties. However, it is important that further research should continue exploring different bacterial strains or other potential inducers for a more comprehensive understanding of this disease model's complexities. Overall, our study underscores the importance of genetic background in determining susceptibility to endotoxin-induced uveitis within mice models and provides valuable insights into factors influencing inflammatory responses. texts.full_text: were unfounded. They concluded that we were just an RIA firm that doesn’t want to be audited. By way of background, I grew up in the brokerage industry. My grandfather founded the brokerage firm Dayton Haigney & Co. (which later became Haigney Securities, Inc.) in Boston in 1936. My father took over the firm in 1980 and I worked there for several years until the firm was sold in the early 1990s. Subsequently, I worked for Merrill Lynch as an institutional equity salesman and then in 2009 I founded my firm, EL CAP, Inc., a registered investment adviser based in Vermont. My family’s broker-dealer was one of the last small full reserve, self-clearing brokerage firms. We were members of FINRA (then known as the NASD) and I was involved in all aspects of our firm’s operations, including financial reporting, regulatory filings and audits. My perspective in this debate crosseMy background deeply informs my stance on issues related to regulation within finance. Given that both sides—FINRA regulators who are advocating for expanded oversight authority over RIAs (Registered Investment Advisers) as well as those of us working directly with clients—are engaged, it’s important not just from a historical but also practical standpoint.
